settings.title: Settings
settings.interface.note: Changes apply immediately when you click Apply or OK.
settings.features.translation_response_c: Reuse a recent identical translation without calling the API.
settings.features.ui_system_tray_note: Closing the window hides to tray instead of quitting.
settings.features.ui_autostart_note: Adds QuickLingo to the Windows startup programs.
settings.features.hotkey_note: Example: <ctrl>+<shift>+t. Requires restart of hotkey listener (toggle feature).
settings.features.input_replace_in_place: After translating selection, paste result back (Windows only).
settings.features.learning_ai_corpus_ana: Analyze tagged history with LLM to build flashcards.
settings.features.privacy_encrypted_keys: Keys are encrypted for this Windows user account. Re-enter keys after OS reinstall.
settings.features.help_missing: No detailed description is available for this option yet.
settings.features.help.ui_always_on_top:
Keeps the QuickLingo window above other applications so you can see translations while working in another app.

Useful when translating side-by-side with a browser, editor, or chat. The window stays visible until you turn this off or minimize the app.
settings.features.help.ui_remember_geome:
Saves the window size and screen position when you close QuickLingo and restores them on the next launch.

If you move the app to a second monitor or resize it for your workflow, you won't need to arrange it again each time.
settings.features.help.ui_remember_zoom:
Remembers the text zoom level (font scale) in the main translation view between sessions.

Helpful if you prefer larger text for readability or a compact layout on a small screen.
settings.features.help.ui_auto_copy_resu:
After each successful translation, automatically copies the translated text to the Windows clipboard.

You can paste the result into any app without selecting and copying manually. The original clipboard content is replaced with the translation.
settings.features.help.ui_system_tray:
Shows QuickLingo in the system tray (notification area). Closing the main window hides the app instead of quitting it.

Use the tray icon or the show/hide hotkey to bring the window back. Global hotkeys can keep working while the window is hidden.
settings.features.help.ui_autostart:
Starts QuickLingo automatically when you log in to Windows.

The app launches in the background according to your tray and window settings. You can disable this anytime without uninstalling.
settings.features.help.history_auto_save:
Saves every completed translation to the local history database.

Records include source text, result, direction, provider, and timestamp. Disable if you don't want translations stored on disk.
settings.features.help.history_search:
Enables text search across saved translation history.

Find past translations by words in the source or result. Search works together with filters and tags when those features are enabled.
settings.features.help.history_filters:
Adds filters in the History window: date range, direction, provider, starred items, and tags.

Narrows long history lists so you can review or export only the records you care about.
settings.features.help.history_export:
Lets you export history records to a CSV file from the History window.

Useful for backups, spreadsheets, or sharing a subset of translations. Export respects active filters when applied.
settings.features.help.history_dashboard:
Shows a bar chart of how many translations you saved per day over the last 30 days.

Open via Tools → Dashboard. Helps spot study or work patterns in your corpus.
settings.features.help.history_model_sta:
Shows a pie chart of which LLM models were used for your saved translations.

Useful to see provider mix and cost drivers. Displayed in Tools → Dashboard.
settings.features.help.history_tags:
Allows assigning tags to history entries and filtering by tag.

Tags help group translations by project, topic, or language pair. Use Tag wizard in History to add, replace, or remove tags on all visible or selected records.
settings.features.help.history_meeting_t:
Builds a chronological transcript from history entries within a configurable time gap.

Entries closer together than the session gap are treated as one meeting or conversation. Export combines them into a readable document.
settings.features.help.learning_phrasebo:
Adds a star (phrasebook) marker on history items for quick access to important phrases.

Starred entries can be filtered in History so you can build a personal phrasebook from real translations you use.
settings.features.help.learning_word_fre:
Analyzes word frequency in your translation history and shows the most common terms.

Helps identify vocabulary you translate often. The "Top words (N)" setting controls how many words appear in the report.
settings.features.help.learning_difficul:
Detects words or phrases that appear often or may be hard based on your history patterns.

Use this as a preview before generating flashcards or focusing study on recurring trouble spots.
settings.features.help.learning_ai_corpu:
Uses an LLM to analyze a batch of tagged history and suggest vocabulary for flashcards.

Configure max candidates and batch size to balance quality, speed, and API cost. Results appear in the Learning window for review before saving cards.
settings.features.help.learning_anki_pre:
Shows generated flashcards inside QuickLingo before you export or study them.

Lets you edit or discard cards so only useful items reach Anki or daily review.
settings.features.help.learning_anki_exp:
Exports learning cards to Anki as a ready-to-import .apkg package or CSV (front/back columns).

For .apkg: File → Import in Anki. For CSV: File → Import. Deck name and tags follow your tag or direction settings when deck scope is enabled.
settings.features.help.learning_deck_sco:
Organizes cards into decks or scopes by history tag and/or translation direction.

Keeps vocabulary separated by project or language pair instead of one mixed deck.
settings.features.help.learning_daily_re:
Runs spaced-repetition review inside QuickLingo.

With FSRS off: Again / Good (simple intervals). With FSRS on: Again / Hard / Good / Easy using the FSRS algorithm. The daily limit caps due cards per session.
settings.features.help.learning_srs_revi:
Switches daily review from simple Again/Good intervals to full FSRS scheduling with four grades.

Requires Daily review to be enabled. Card scheduling state is stored locally in the learning database.
settings.features.help.learning_streak:
Tracks consecutive days with at least one completed review.

Motivates regular practice. The streak resets if you skip a day without finishing the minimum review.
settings.features.help.learning_extract_:
Adds a context action in History to send a selected word or phrase into a learning deck.

Quick way to turn a translation you just looked up into a flashcard without retyping.
settings.features.help.translation_respo:
Reuses a recent identical translation from history instead of calling the API again.

Matches source text, direction, and relevant settings. Cache entries expire after the configured TTL in days.
settings.features.help.translation_conte:
Sends the last N translations from history as context to the LLM for more consistent wording.

Helps with ongoing documents or chats where terminology should stay aligned across segments.
settings.features.help.translation_gloss:
Injects your custom glossary terms into translation prompts so fixed terms translate the same way every time.

Manage entries on the Glossary settings tab. Applies when the active direction matches a glossary rule.
settings.features.help.translation_strea:
Shows translation text as it arrives from the LLM instead of waiting for the full response.

During streaming the result appears as plain text; formatting is applied when complete. Cache hits are still instant.
settings.features.help.translation_reque:
Processes translation requests one at a time in order instead of running several API calls in parallel.

Reduces rate-limit errors and keeps results predictable when you trigger many translations quickly.
settings.features.help.input_global_hotk:
Global hotkey that copies the current text selection in any app and translates it in QuickLingo.

Configure the key combo below (pynput format, e.g. <ctrl>+<shift>+t). Toggle this option off and on to reload the listener after changing the combo.

Global hotkey that translates whatever is currently on the clipboard without selecting text.

Useful when copy is easier than a selection shortcut. Uses the same combo format as the selection hotkey.
settings.features.help.input_double_ctrl:
Detects two Ctrl+C presses in quick succession and starts a clipboard translation.

Works in apps where a dedicated hotkey is awkward but double-copy is natural. Does not replace normal single Ctrl+C behavior.
settings.features.help.input_replace_in_:
After translating selected text via hotkey, pastes the translation back into the original field (Windows only).

Simulates select → translate → paste in one step. Some apps may block simulated input for security.
settings.features.help.privacy_encrypted:
Stores API keys encrypted with Windows DPAPI for the current user account.

Keys are not saved as plain text on disk. After an OS reinstall or new Windows profile, you must enter keys again in Settings → API Keys.
learning.word_freq_disabled: Word frequency is disabled in Settings → Features.

settings.models.intro: Choose which models appear in the model dropdown on the main window and in Learning. Order matches the dropdown order. Filter by provider, pick from the list, or type a model ID manually.
settings.models.add_placeholder: Model ID or pick from list
settings.models.error_duplicate: This model is already in the list.
